docs.text: Here, I share my technical projects and experiences to collaborate with other enthusiasts seeking practical guides and innovative solutions.

docs.title: TensorFlow GPU Setup in WSL2
docs.text:
Native GPU Support on Windows is Deprecated: TensorFlow 2.10 is the last version to support GPU natively on Windows.

From 2.11 onward:

  • Native GPU support was removed

  • CUDA-based GPU usage only works on Linux or WSL2

docs.title: ✅ Set Up NVIDIA GPU in WSL2
✅ Install TensorFlow in WSL2 (with GPU)
docs.text:
uv venv
source .venv/bin/activate
uv pip install tensorflow # (1)!
  1. The latest tensorflow from PyPI on Linux includes GPU support out of the box, but for stability select tensorflow 2.15.

docs.title: ✅ Test GPU Access
docs.text:
Create a gpu_check.py like this:

import tensorflow as tf

print("TensorFlow Version:", tf.__version__)
print("GPUs:", tf.config.list_physical_devices('GPU'))

Then execute it using:

>>> python gpu_check.py
TensorFlow Version: 2.15.0
GPUs: [PhysicalDevice(name='/physical_device:GPU:0', device_type='GPU')]

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
Most of my projects revolve around the versatility and power of Python, making it my go-to programming language for tackling a wide range of challenges.

Whether it's automating workflows, building intelligent chatbots, or crafting efficient scripts, Python stands at the core of my work.

docs.title: Virtual Environment
docs.text:
Create a virtual environment with:

venvuv 
python -m venv .venv

uv venv .venv # (1)!

  1. uv venv .venv ensures that all virtual environments are created with the name .venv. To use the current directory name as the virtual environment's name, run:

    uv venv

Activate the virtual environment with:

PowerShellBash 
.\.venv\Scripts\activate

source .venv/Script/activate

Install dependencies with:

uvpip 
uv pip install -r requirements.txt

pip install -r requirements.txt

When working on Python projects, it’s crucial to ensure that dependencies are consistent across different environments. A requirements.txt file helps achieve this by specifying the exact versions of packages used in your project.

uvpip 
uv pip freeze > requirements.txt

pip freeze > requirements.txt

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Construction Defects Detection Chatbot is a YOLO11 model designed to identify construction defects from images. The model categorizes defects into three classes:

  • broken-brick

  • drip-mortar

  • lintel-damage

docs.title: Steps to Install
docs.text:
Create and activate a virtual environment:

uv venv
source .venv/Scripts/activate

Install the required dependencies:

uv pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Backend
docs.text:
Run the FastAPI development server with the following command:

fastapi dev app.py
